info.title: Chapter Web Services
info.description: Public APIs provided in 3DSpace to manage Chapter objects (dsreq)

post.summary: Creates Chapters
post.description: Creates Chapters using payload items. A maximum of 10 items is allowed.

parameters.name: ENO_CSRF_TOKEN
parameters.description: csrf token. You can get it from 3DSpace/resources/v1/application/CSRF

parameters.name: Content-Type
parameters.description: It advertises which content types the client is able to understand

parameters.name: $mask
parameters.description: Mask defining what will be returned. Default Mask is:dsreq:ChapterBaseMask.<br>dsreq:ChapterMask.Details is deprecated and is replaced by dsreq:ChapterPublicMask.Details (see details in the schema definition of the response).

parameters.name: $fields
parameters.description: We can add several fields with comma(,) separator to get them in the response.<br>More documentation here: Web Services and Events | 3DSpace | Common Principles | Corpus Web Service Principles

parameters.name: $mva
parameters.description: To return multi-values attributes with array format. default is a sring format with values separated by special character.<br>More documentation here: Web Services and Events | 3DSpace | Common Principles | Support of Multi Valuated Attributes in corpus web Services

200.description: Request successful.

schema.example: Response will be returned with oneOf mask available in the Schema Tab.

400.description: BAD REQUEST. The request could not be understood by the server due to malformed syntax. The client SHOULD NOT repeat the request without modifications.

401.description: UNAUTHORIZED. The request requires user authentication. The response MUST include a WWW-Authenticate header field containing a challenge applicable to the requested resource. The client MAY repeat the request with a suitable Authorization header field. If the request already included Authorization credentials, then the 401 response indicates that authorization has been refused for those credentials. If the 401 response contains the same challenge as the prior response, and the user agent has already attempted authentication at least once, then the user SHOULD be presented the entity that was given in the response, since that entity might include relevant diagnostic information. HTTP access authentication is explained in "HTTP Authentication: Basic and Digest Access Authentication".

403.description: FORBIDDEN. The server understood the request, but is refusing to fulfill it. Authorization will not help and the request SHOULD NOT be repeated. If the request method was not HEAD and the server wishes to make public why the request has not been fulfilled, it SHOULD describe the reason for the refusal in the entity. If the server does not wish to make this information available to the client, the status code 404 (Not Found) can be used instead.

500.description: INTERNAL SERVER ERROR. A generic error message, given when an unexpected condition was encountered and no more specific message is suitable.

patch.description: Modifies the Chapter with an attributes list.

get.description: Gets a Chapter with an attributes list.

get.summary: Gets a list of Chapters from indexed search
get.description: Gets a list of Chapters. By default, returns a total of up to 50 items, can be optionally increased up to 1000 items using $top query parameter. Recommendation: Use $searchStr query parameter for better performances

parameters.name: $searchStr
parameters.description: Provide a 3DEXPERIENCE search expression with a minimum of two characters. Note that this will run on indexed data only.<br>Example1 searchStr="FYD" OR (modified>"2021-09-01T00:00:00Z" AND modified<"2021-09-23T00:00:00Z")<br>Example2 searchStr="ARB" AND modified<"2019-03-19T08:00:00.000Z"

parameters.name: $mask
parameters.description: Mask defining what will be returned. Default Mask is:dsreq:ChapterBaseMask

parameters.name: $top
parameters.description: Represents the total number of items returned from the search, accepts a maximum value of 1000.

parameters.name: $skip
parameters.description: Represents the number of items to skip (to be used along with $top query parameter)

200.description: Request successful.

400.description: BAD REQUEST. The request could not be understood by the server due to malformed syntax. The client SHOULD NOT repeat the request without modifications.

401.description: UNAUTHORIZED. The request requires user authentication. The response MUST include a WWW-Authenticate header field containing a challenge applicable to the requested resource. The client MAY repeat the request with a suitable Authorization header field. If the request already included Authorization credentials, then the 401 response indicates that authorization has been refused for those credentials. If the 401 response contains the same challenge as the prior response, and the user agent has already attempted authentication at least once, then the user SHOULD be presented the entity that was given in the response, since that entity might include relevant diagnostic information. HTTP access authentication is explained in "HTTP Authentication: Basic and Digest Access Authentication".

500.description: INTERNAL SERVER ERROR. A generic error message, given when an unexpected condition was encountered and no more specific message is suitable.

post.summary: Get multiple Chapter Items which are indexed
post.description: Get multiple Chapter Items which are Indexed. API Works only for Indexed Data. Maximum of 1000 items can be passed to fetch the information

parameters.name: $mask
parameters.description: Mask defining what will be returned. Default Mask is:dskern:Mask.Default

parameters.name: $fields
parameters.description: We can add several fields with comma(,) separator to get in the response.

parameters.name: $mva
parameters.description: To return multi-values attributes with array format. default is a sring format with values separated by special character

200.description: Request successful (Beware, properties with empty values are not seen in the response).

207.description: Request successful but with multiple status. The Physical IDs that could not be fetched due to [ DATA NOT INDEXED, INVALID ID] are listed in the response inside an array named nonmembers.

400.description: BAD REQUEST. The request could not be understood by the server due to malformed syntax. The client SHOULD NOT repeat the request without modifications.

401.description: UNAUTHORIZED. The request requires user authentication. The response MUST include a WWW-Authenticate header field containing a challenge applicable to the requested resource. The client MAY repeat the request with a suitable Authorization header field. If the request already included Authorization credentials, then the 401 response indicates that authorization has been refused for those credentials. If the 401 response contains the same challenge as the prior response, and the user agent has already attempted authentication at least once, then the user SHOULD be presented the entity that was given in the response, since that entity might include relevant diagnostic information. HTTP access authentication is explained in "HTTP Authentication: Basic and Digest Access Authentication".

500.description: INTERNAL SERVER ERROR. A generic error message, given when an unexpected condition was encountered and no more specific message is suitable.

post.summary: Expands a Chapter structure based on the expandDepth using indexed queries.
post.description: Expands a Chapter structure based on the expandDepth using indexed queries. By default expandDepth is 0 (Maximum is 10, -1 for all).<br> At maximum, 10000 paths can be fetched, an error will be generated otherwise (code 413).<br> The totalItems field in the response payload is the sum of three elements: the objects (no dupe), the connections and the paths.

parameters.name: ENO_CSRF_TOKEN
parameters.description: csrf token. You can get it from 3DSpace/resources/v1/application/CSRF

400.description: BAD REQUEST. The request could not be understood by the server due to malformed syntax. The client SHOULD NOT repeat the request without modifications.

401.description: UNAUTHORIZED. The request requires user authentication. The response MUST include a WWW-Authenticate header field containing a challenge applicable to the requested resource. The client MAY repeat the request with a suitable Authorization header field. If the request already included Authorization credentials, then the 401 response indicates that authorization has been refused for those credentials. If the 401 response contains the same challenge as the prior response, and the user agent has already attempted authentication at least once, then the user SHOULD be presented the entity that was given in the response, since that entity might include relevant diagnostic information. HTTP access authentication is explained in "HTTP Authentication: Basic and Digest Access Authentication".

413.description: Content Too Large. Too many paths in the response (Paths limit is set to 10 000 elements)

500.description: INTERNAL SERVER ERROR. A generic error message, given when an unexpected condition was encountered and no more specific message is suitable.

200.description: OK. The request has succeeded. The information returned with the response is dependent on the method used in the request, for example: GET an entity corresponding to the requested resource is sent in the response; HEAD the entity-header fields corresponding to the requested resource are sent in the response without any message-body; POST an entity describing or containing the result of the action; TRACE an entity containing the request message as received by the end server.

post.summary: Creates a Chapter Usage below a Chapter
post.description: Creates Chapter Usages using payload instances. A maximum of 10 usages is allowed.

parameters.description: csrf token. You can get it from 3DSpace/resources/v1/application/CSRF

parameters.name: DS-Configuration-Authoring-Context
parameters.description: Work Under Evolution. Will be ignored if DS-Change-Authoring-Context is set

parameters.name: $fields
parameters.description: We can add several fields with comma(,) separator to get them in the response.

parameters.name: $mask
parameters.description: Mask defining what will be returned. Default Mask is:dsreq:ChapterUsageMask.Details

post.summary: Service to set the effectivity variant expression (XML) on a single relationship.
post.description: Service to set the effectivity variant expression (XML) on a single relationship. If setVariant service is executed under Work Under (Change Action) then it may lead to a new evolution of existing relationship. WARNING: Coherency between Evolution and Variant Expression are under users responsibility.<br><br>Please find below the list of possible error codes and error messages: <br> 102 : The parent reference of the given instance does not have a Configuration Context. <br>117 : The input effectivity expression or data used in the expression is not correct. <br>118 : Instance's identifier in the input is not valid or correct. <br>119 : Effectivity cannot be set due to dictionary data. <br>120 : The criteria is not enabled on the parent reference. <br>123 : Model provided in the input expression is not part of Configuration Context. <br>124 : Model provided in the input expression is not accessible or does not exist. <br>125 : Input expression not well-formatted (No model found). <br>126 : The criteria used in the input expression is not enabled on parent reference. <br>127 : Error occured during the save of the new effectivities. <br>128 : Error occured during the update of Configuration Revision effectivities. <br>129 : Effectivities cannot be set because changing at least one of them would impact frozen evolution range. <br>130 : The parent reference is not configurable. <br>199 : Failure detected during operation. <br><br>Multiple effectivity expression (like OR & AND expression) is not supported for Chapters. <br>

parameters.name: ENO_CSRF_TOKEN
parameters.description: csrf token. You can get it from 3DSpace/resources/v1/application/CSRF

200.description: OK. The request has succeeded. The information returned with the response is dependent on the method used in the request, for example: GET an entity corresponding to the requested resource is sent in the response; HEAD the entity-header fields corresponding to the requested resource are sent in the response without any message-body; POST an entity describing or containing the result of the action; TRACE an entity containing the request message as received by the end server.

errorCode.description: The value of this field will be a valid error code. This field will be available only if set variant failed for a input relationship. In case of set variant success then this field will not be available in output.

message.example: The parent reference of the given instance does not have a Configuration Context.
message.description: The value of this field will be a valid NLS error message. This field will be available only if set variant failed for a input relationship. In case of set variant success then this field will not be available in output.

errorDetails.example: The reference : F6AF82561E5700005EB123650003C100 does not have a model attached.
errorDetails.description: The value of this field will be the details about the error. This field will be available only if set variant failed for a input relationship. In case of set variant success then this field will not be available in output.

id.description: The value of this field will be physical id of a relationship. If there is a error in set variant effectivity then this field will not be available in output.
The physical id of a newly evolved relationship.

post.description: Service to set the effectivity variant expression (XML) on a single relationship. If setVariant service is executed under Work Under (Change Action) then it may lead to a new evolution of existing relationship. WARNING: Coherency between Evolution and Variant Expression are under users responsibility.<br><br>Please find below the list of possible error codes and error messages: <br> 102 : The parent reference of the given instance does not have a Configuration Context. <br>117 : The input effectivity expression or data used in the expression is not correct. <br>118 : Instance's identifier in the input is not valid or correct. <br>119 : Effectivity cannot be set due to dictionary data. <br>120 : The criteria is not enabled on the parent reference. <br>123 : Model provided in the input expression is not part of Configuration Context. <br>124 : Model provided in the input expression is not accessible or does not exist. <br>125 : Input expression not well-formatted (No model found). <br>126 : The criteria used in the input expression is not enabled on parent reference. <br>127 : Error occured during the save of the new effectivities. <br>128 : Error occured during the update of Configuration Revision effectivities. <br>129 : Effectivities cannot be set because changing at least one of them would impact frozen evolution range. <br>130 : The parent reference is not configurable. <br>199 : Failure detected during operation. <br><br>Multiple effectivity expression (like OR & AND expression) is not supported for Chapters. <br>

parameters.name: ENO_CSRF_TOKEN
parameters.description: csrf token. You can get it from 3DSpace/resources/v1/application/CSRF

200.description: OK. The request has succeeded. The information returned with the response is dependent on the method used in the request, for example: GET an entity corresponding to the requested resource is sent in the response; HEAD the entity-header fields corresponding to the requested resource are sent in the response without any message-body; POST an entity describing or containing the result of the action; TRACE an entity containing the request message as received by the end server.

errorCode.description: The value of this field will be a valid error code. This field will be available only if set variant failed for a input relationship. In case of set variant success then this field will not be available in output.

message.example: The parent reference of the given instance does not have a Configuration Context.
message.description: The value of this field will be a valid NLS error message. This field will be available only if set variant failed for a input relationship. In case of set variant success then this field will not be available in output.

errorDetails.example: The reference : F6AF82561E5700005EB123650003C100 does not have a model attached.
errorDetails.description: The value of this field will be the details about the error. This field will be available only if set variant failed for a input relationship. In case of set variant success then this field will not be available in output.

id.description: The value of this field will be physical id of a relationship. If there is a error in set variant effectivity then this field will not be available in output.
The physical id of a newly evolved relationship.

400.description: BAD REQUEST. The request could not be understood by the server due to malformed syntax. The client SHOULD NOT repeat the request without modifications.

401.description: UNAUTHORIZED. The request requires user authentication. The response MUST include a WWW-Authenticate header field containing a challenge applicable to the requested resource. The client MAY repeat the request with a suitable Authorization header field. If the request already included Authorization credentials, then the 401 response indicates that authorization has been refused for those credentials. If the 401 response contains the same challenge as the prior response, and the user agent has already attempted authentication at least once, then the user SHOULD be presented the entity that was given in the response, since that entity might include relevant diagnostic information. HTTP access authentication is explained in "HTTP Authentication: Basic and Digest Access Authentication".

500.description: INTERNAL SERVER ERROR. A generic error message, given when an unexpected condition was encountered and no more specific message is suitable.

403.description: FORBIDDEN. The server understood the request, but is refusing to fulfill it. Authorization will not help and the request SHOULD NOT be repeated. If the request method was not HEAD and the server wishes to make public why the request has not been fulfilled, it SHOULD describe the reason for the refusal in the entity. If the server does not wish to make this information available to the client, the status code 404 (Not Found) can be used instead.

The information returned with the response is dependent on the method used in the request, for example: GET an entity corresponding to the requested resource is sent in the response; HEAD the entity-header fields corresponding to the requested resource are sent in the response without any message-body; POST an entity describing or containing the result of the action; TRACE an entity containing the request message as received by the end server.

id.description: The physical id of a relationship.

400.description: BAD REQUEST. The request could not be understood by the server due to malformed syntax. The client SHOULD NOT repeat the request without modifications.

401.description: UNAUTHORIZED. The request requires user authentication. The response MUST include a WWW-Authenticate header field containing a challenge applicable to the requested resource. The client MAY repeat the request with a suitable Authorization header field. If the request already included Authorization credentials, then the 401 response indicates that authorization has been refused for those credentials. If the 401 response contains the same challenge as the prior response, and the user agent has already attempted authentication at least once, then the user SHOULD be presented the entity that was given in the response, since that entity might include relevant diagnostic information. HTTP access authentication is explained in "HTTP Authentication: Basic and Digest Access Authentication".

500.description: INTERNAL SERVER ERROR. A generic error message, given when an unexpected condition was encountered and no more specific message is suitable.

403.description: FORBIDDEN. The server understood the request, but is refusing to fulfill it. Authorization will not help and the request SHOULD NOT be repeated. If the request method was not HEAD and the server wishes to make public why the request has not been fulfilled, it SHOULD describe the reason for the refusal in the entity. If the server does not wish to make this information available to the client, the status code 404 (Not Found) can be used instead.
